title: Graaff-Reinet set to become Robert Sobukwe Town following ministerial approval
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - Graaff-Reinet is expected to undergo a name change to Robert Sobukwe Town, following the approval of a broader series of 21 geographical name changes by the Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture, Gayton McKenzie.","html":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - Graaff-Reinet is expected to undergo a name change to Robert Sobukwe Town, following the approval of a broader series of 21 geographical name changes by the Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture, Gayton McKenzie."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The South African Geographical Names Council has confirmed that these approvals are in place but noted that it has not been finalised as it still needs to be formally gazetted - a process expected to take place in the coming weeks."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Graaff-Reinet is the fourth-oldest town in South Africa, established in 1786 and named after the then Cape Governor, Cornelis Jacob van de Graaff, and his wife, Reinet.","html":"Graaff-Reinet is the fourth-oldest town in South Africa, established in 1786 and named after the then Cape Governor, Cornelis Jacob van de Graaff, and his wife, Reinet."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The proposed new name would honour Robert Sobukwe, an anti-apartheid activist and founding member of the Pan Africanist Congress (PAC).","html":"The proposed new name would honour Robert Sobukwe, an anti-apartheid activist and founding member of the Pan Africanist Congress (PAC)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The proposed renaming of Graaff-Reinet to either Robert Sobukwe or Fred Hufkie, as well as the renaming of three other towns in the Dr Beyers Naudé municipal area - Adendorp to Kwa Mseki Bishop Limba, Aberdeen to Camdeboo, and Nieu-Bethesda to Kwa Noheleni - was announced by Mark Mandita, representative of the Eastern Cape Department of Sports, Recreation, Arts and Culture, during a special council meeting on 29 August 2023.","html":"The proposed renaming of Graaff-Reinet to either Robert Sobukwe or Fred Hufkie, as well as the renaming of three other towns in the Dr Beyers Naudé municipal area - Adendorp to Kwa Mseki Bishop Limba, Aberdeen to Camdeboo, and Nieu-Bethesda to Kwa Noheleni - was announced by Mark Mandita, representative of the Eastern Cape Department of Sports, Recreation, Arts and Culture, during a special council meeting on 29 August 2023."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The proposal was met with strong opposition from local residents and based on this, the national council - SAGNC - declined in 2024 to recommend the name changes.","html":"The proposal was met with strong opposition from local residents and based on this, the national council - SAGNC - declined in 2024 to recommend the name changes."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"At the time, the Eastern Cape provincial council indicated that the matter had not been concluded, explaining that the application had been rejected due to insufficient supporting information, which could be addressed in future submissions.","html":"At the time, the Eastern Cape provincial council indicated that the matter had not been concluded, explaining that the application had been rejected due to insufficient supporting information, which could be addressed in future submissions."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"When Mandita announced the proposed renaming of Dr Beyers Naudé towns he asserted that the Protection of Personal Information (Popi) Act prohibits the department from disclosing the identities of the two applicants who submitted the proposals."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"However, a press statement released by the Azania Movement on Tuesday 27 January revealed that Nolubabalo Mcinga, the president of the movement and a native of Graaff-Reinet, tabled a Motion Without Notice in 2024, calling for the renaming to honour Robert Mangaliso Sobukwe while serving in the National Council of Provinces (NCOP) representing the Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF).","html":"However, a press statement released by the Azania Movement on Tuesday 27 January revealed that Nolubabalo Mcinga, the president of the movement and a native of Graaff-Reinet, tabled a Motion Without Notice in 2024, calling for the renaming to honour Robert Mangaliso Sobukwe while serving in the National Council of Provinces (NCOP) representing the Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"According to the Azania Movement the process faced delays - primarily due to concerns regarding public participation and petitions opposing the renaming - but Mcinga, with the academic support of historian Dr JJ Klaas (PhD, Cambridge University), challenged these delays by engaging directly with the Eastern Cape Geographical Names Committee (ECPGNC), requesting access to petitions, and ensuring that the people’s submissions were fully considered.","html":"According to the Azania Movement the process faced delays - primarily due to concerns regarding public participation and petitions opposing the renaming - but Mcinga, with the academic support of historian Dr JJ Klaas (PhD, Cambridge University), challenged these delays by engaging directly with the Eastern Cape Geographical Names Committee (ECPGNC), requesting access to petitions, and ensuring that the people’s submissions were fully considered."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Subsequently, the deputy chairperson of the South African Geographical Names Council (SAGNC), Johnny Mohlala, confirmed that, during a meeting on 26 January, the ECPGNC submitted the names for standardisation, and Minister Gayton McKenzie formally approved the renaming of Graaff-Reinet to Robert Sobukwe Town, alongside other Eastern Cape name changes.","html":"Subsequently, the deputy chairperson of the South African Geographical Names Council (SAGNC), Johnny Mohlala, confirmed that, during a meeting on 26 January, the ECPGNC submitted the names for standardisation, and Minister Gayton McKenzie formally approved the renaming of Graaff-Reinet to Robert Sobukwe Town, alongside other Eastern Cape name changes."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"“For the people of Robert Sobukwe Town, the renaming corrects a colonial legacy and restores historical dignity,” Mcinga stated."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Our next step will be to advocate for the renaming of the country itself, as ‘South Africa’ is not a proper name but a geographic direction on the continent.”","html":"“Our next step will be to advocate for the renaming of the country itself, as ‘South Africa’ is not a proper name but a geographic direction on the continent.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Graaff-Reinet set to become Robert Sobukwe Town following ministerial approval
